meta.description: Commercial UAS intelligence — mapping/survey, inspection, agriculture, delivery, geospatial, dealer ecosystem, DJI transition, NDAA compliance for commercial operators

records.title: DJI FCC Covered List Action — What Commercial Operators Actually Need to Know
records.summary: FCC action Dec 22 2025: all foreign-made UAS added to Covered List. NOT a flight ban — no grounding order, no remote disablement. Existing authorized DJI aircraft remain flyable. New DJI models cannot receive FCC authorization. Practical impact: no fleet expansion using DJI, replacement parts path closing, firmware updates may stop. Commercial operators on federally funded projects (FHWA, DOT, FEMA) cannot procure or operate DJI under ASDA. Private-funded operations face no federal restriction on existing fleet. Parallel deployment strategy: maintain DJI for current work, onboard NDAA-compliant alternatives for grant-funded projects.

records.title: NASAO White Paper — Commercial Survey/Mapping/Inspection Fleet Exposure by State
records.summary: NASAO white paper commercial impact: 467 airframes grounded across state transportation and aviation agencies — primarily survey, mapping, construction inspection, and infrastructure documentation fleets. These are commercial workflows (photogrammetry, LiDAR, Pix4D/TBC integration) running on federal funding. Replacement cost $20–60K per airframe vs $15K DJI. Commercial survey firms working on federally funded DOT/FHWA projects are equally bound by ASDA as the agencies themselves. Idaho benchmark: $15K DJI → $42K compliant replacement (2.8x premium).
records.paywall_reason: Free tier — public white paper summary

records.title: Trimble Dealer Network DJI Problem — Why the Incumbent Advisor Can't Help
records.summary: Seiler, Frontier Precision, and Duncan-Parnell are all large-format Trimble geospatial dealers whose UAS revenue is built on DJI bundled with Trimble RTK/TBC/Pix4D workflows. DJI was the drone that completed the Trimble field collection stack. That stack is now broken at the hardware layer. None of these dealers can credibly advise on replacement — they sold customers into the problem. UAS revenue is 15-35% of total for major Trimble dealers. Trimble itself has not released a branded NDAA-compliant drone. Dealers are on their own.

records.title: NDAA-Compliant Commercial Platforms — Replacement Matrix for Survey/Mapping/Inspection
records.summary: Top NDAA-compliant alternatives for commercial survey/mapping/inspection workflows: Wingtra (VTOL fixed-wing, best large-area coverage), Freefly Astro (modular, LiDAR-capable, Blue UAS), Inspired Flight IF800/IF1200A (heavy lift, Blue UAS), ACSL SOTEN (compact, NDAA-compliant Japanese, $7–14K — most affordable option), WISPR SkyScout 2+ (Green UAS certified, Mississippi-made), Quantum-Systems Trinity F90+ (fixed-wing VTOL mapping). Cost gap narrowing but 2–4x premium persists. Pix4D and TBC software compatibility varies by platform.

records.title: Frontier Precision — Most Advanced Trimble Dealer Pivot, NDAA Platform Catalog
records.summary: Frontier Precision (est. 1988, Bismarck ND, employee-owned, 22+ states, 9 Trimble Certified Service Centers) is the most advanced of the three major Trimble dealers in transitioning to NDAA-compliant platforms. Current NDAA catalog: Skydio, WISPR, Inspired Flight, ACSL SOTEN, Freefly, Quantum-Systems — alongside continued DJI sales. Published NDAA transition guides. Inspired Flight partner. Still DJI dealer but actively advising customers on transition. Channel partner candidate for Forge commercial mapping intelligence.

records.title: AirData Public Safety Program — Fleet Management and Transparency Tool
records.summary: AirData launched Public Safety Program March 2026: 444K+ pilots, 232 countries, 60M+ flights. Public Portal provides residents searchable drone flight records — Chula Vista PD, Las Vegas Metro PD, Sacramento PD, Huntsville PD, Ohio DOT all using it. Fusus and Milestone integration for command dashboards. Multi-drone live view. Dock-based DFR support. Transparency tool for community relations. Key for commercial fleet operators needing compliance reporting on federally funded projects.

records.title: AUVSI Green UAS Certification — Commercial Alternative to Blue UAS
records.summary: AUVSI Green UAS Certification: industry-led cybersecurity and supply chain vetting program for non-DoD/commercial operators. Complements Blue UAS for public safety and commercial procurement. Green UAS is not government-mandated but increasingly required by agencies using federal grants. WISPR SkyScout 2+ recently achieved Green UAS certification. Sits alongside FCC Conditional Approval (March 2026, 4 systems, expires Dec 31 2026) as a compliance pathway for commercial operators on federal projects.

records.title: DJI 96% of Detected US Drone Platforms — ASSURE A83 2025 Annual Report
records.summary: FAA-funded ASSURE A83 2025 report: analyzed Remote ID telemetry from 64 monitoring locations nationwide. DJI represents 96% of detected US drone platforms. Combined with NASAO fleet grounding data: the platforms enforcing geofencing and altitude compliance are being replaced. 58% of flights in UAS Facility Map areas detected above altitude limits — safety compliance gap opening as DJI exits market. Commercial operators replacing DJI are transitioning to platforms with less integrated airspace enforcement.

records.title: Everdrone — Medical Drone Delivery Commercial Model, SEK 36M Raised
records.summary: Everdrone (Gothenburg, Sweden) raised SEK 36M March 2026 to expand commercial drone emergency medical services. Operational in Västra Götaland, Stockholm (Sweden), Normandy France (via SAMU), UK (KSS Air Ambulance). New E3 drone: 24kg, 80km/h, 4.5kg payload, SORA-certified BVLOS in urban areas. GridPlanner coverage analysis tool for new markets. Commercial model: healthcare regions procure service, Everdrone operates autonomously. 10 Skybases target by end 2026 covering 25% of Västra Götaland population. Clinical trial with Karolinska Institutet 2026.

records.title: DJI Fleet Grounding — Balanced Responsibility Chain: Congress, DJI, Dealers, Agencies
records.summary: Responsibility distribution: Congress (HIGH) — created ASDA with Dec 2025 cutoff in Dec 2023, two years notice, no transition funding, $25M for $200M+ in Florida. DJI (HIGH) — declined security audit, 96% market share left no viable alternative ecosystem, 2-4x price premium. Dealers (MEDIUM) — clear statutory signals from Dec 2023 forward, most failed to proactively advise customers; Seiler most exposed. Agencies (MEDIUM) — public signals available, procurement cycles and budget timing made response difficult. FAA/OMB/FCC (MEDIUM) — OMB M-26-02 arrived 30 days before cutoff. NDAA manufacturers (LOW/CONSTRUCTIVE) — production capacity hasn't scaled to meet demand.
